revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Doburoku Matsuri]][[ja:どぶろく祭り]]
{{Short description|Japanese festival|qq=}} '''Doburoku Matsuri''' or '''Doburoku Festival''' is a {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|qid=Q132241|lt=Festival|1=Festival}} held at {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}s and other places where {{ill|Nigori|en|Nigori|qid=Q60995819|lt=doburoku|1=Nigori}} is served. {{ill|Nigori|en|Nigori|qid=Q60995819|lt=Doburoku|1=Nigori}} is a Japanese [[sake]] that holds religious significance. Sanctified Doburoku is called [[Omiki]] and is accompanied by a series of rituals. In Japan, it has been customary since ancient times to make doburoku and offer it to the gods to bring a good harvest in the coming year. Move than 40 shrines hold {{ill|Doburoku Matsuri|ja|どぶろく祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doburoku Matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to どぶろく祭り|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to どぶろく祭り}}.<ref name="SakeNo09">{{Cite web |title=酒類総合研究所情報誌「お酒のはなし」第9号 |url=https://www.nrib.go.jp/sake/pdf/SakeNo09.pdf |publisher=[[酒類総合研究所]] |accessdate=2019-01-21 |archive-date=2018-03-04 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180304090922/http://www.nrib.go.jp/sake/pdf/SakeNo09.pdf |url-status=dead |qq=}}</ref> As a rule, it is forbidden to take doburoku out of the premises of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}.<ref name="SakeNo09" /> == Shirahigetawara Shrine == The [[Shirahigetawara Shrine]] Doburoku Festival has continued since {{ill|710|en|710|qid=Q23518|lt=710|1=710}}, and is held annually on {{ill|October 17|en|October 17|qid=Q2962|lt=October 17|1=October 17}} and {{ill|October 18|en|October 18|qid=Q2964|lt=18|1=October 18}}.<ref>[http://www.kit-suki.com/enjoy/index.php?action=story&sub_cat_id=3 お祭りやイベント：楽しむ] 杵築市観光協会</ref> In recent years{{When|date=June 2022|qq=}}, many areas have been designated as {{ill|Nigori|en|Nigori|qid=Q60995819|lt=Doburoku Special Zones|1=Nigori}} where the production of doburoku is allowed. Earlier, the shrine was the only one in [[Kyushu]] to be authorized by the National Tax Agency.<ref name="jr-odekake">{{Cite web |url=http://guide.jr-odekake.net/event/26673 |title=白鬚田原神社 どぶろく祭り：九州エリア |access-date=2022-06-17 |archive-date=2015-10-03 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151003220345/http://guide.jr-odekake.net/event/26673 |url-status=bot: unknown |qq=}} JRおでかけネット</ref> A pre-festival is held on the day before Doburoku Matsuri on October 17. Doburoku is served on both days, and on the 18th, a [[mikoshi]] parade is held. In addition, a "Beard Boast Contest" is held in two categories, one for black hair and the other for beards, named after the shrine.<ref>[http://www.47news.jp/localnews/oita/2014/10/post_20141018051804.html 美しく、長く…鬚自慢 杵築の白鬚田原神社]{{Dead link|date=December 2023 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes |qq=}} 47NEWS（よんななニュース）、2014th year 10月18日</ref> The series of events that began on September 25 and continued until October 18 were designated as Kunitoh no toya event by the government on December 20, 1984 (Showa 59) as an {{ill|Intangible folk cultural property for which measures such as record making should be taken|ja|記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible folk cultural property for which measures such as record making should be taken|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11642584}}.<ref>[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161301 国東のとうや行事] - [[文化遺産オンライン]]（[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]]）</ref><ref>[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/312/577 国東のとうや行事] - 国指定文化財等データベース（[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]]）</ref> It was also selected as a Doburoku Festival of Shirahige Shrine by [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]] on March 23, 1971, as a ''Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property.'' <ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/ootatiiki-siteibunnkazaiitirann.html 大田地域 指定文化財一覧] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305161402/http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/ootatiiki-siteibunnkazaiitirann.html |date=2016-03-05 |qq=}} 杵築市</ref><ref>[http://www.47news.jp/localnews/oita/2014/09/post_20140925174904.html うまい一杯になあれ 白鬚田原神社でどぶろく仕込み - 大分のニュース]{{Dead link|date=December 2023 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes |qq=}} 47NEWS（よんななニュース）、2014th year 9月25日</ref> == Nagakusa Tenjin Shrine == The {{ill|Nigori|en|Nigori|qid=Q60995819|lt=Doburoku|1=Nigori}} Festival at [[Nagakusa Tenjin Shrine]] is a {{ill|Festival|en|Festival|qid=Q132241|lt=Ritual|1=Festival}} held annually, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City.<ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead |qq=}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社|qq=}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社|qq=}}</ref> The Doburoku festival there is said to date back to {{ill|1494|en|1494|qid=Q6693|lt=1494|1=1494}} ({{ill|Meiō|en|Meiō|qid=Q1193841|lt=Meiō|1=Meiō}}3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was served at an annual festival to parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /> Fujita Tamibu donated about 990 {{ill|Square metre|en|Square metre|qid=Q25343|lt=m<sup>2</sup>|1=Square metre}} in {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}}5 ({{ill|1665|en|1665|qid=Q7582|lt=1665|1=1665}}). Brewing was then suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to epidemic disease in the village, brewing resumed in {{ill|1668|en|1668|qid=Q7595|lt=1668|1=1668}} (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124" /> This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time. === Brewing === {{ill|Brewing|en|Brewing|qid=Q869095|lt=Brewing|1=Brewing}} takes place on the temple grounds from early January, with the task rotated among six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /> After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the {{ill|Ujiko|en|Ujiko|qid=Q125562175|lt=Ujiko|1=Ujiko}} (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /> {{ill|Shōjō|en|Shōjō|qid=Q3042028|lt=Shōjō|1=Shōjō}}, an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /> Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the event.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022|qq=}} == Gifu prefecture == {{ill|Iijima Hachimangu|ja|飯島八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iijima Hachimangu}}, {{ill|Shirakawa Hachimangu|ja|白川八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Hachimangu|1=Shirakawa Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579699}} and other shrines in celebrate in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. ==See also== *{{ill|Nigori|en|Nigori|qid=Q60995819|lt=Doburoku#Japanese doburoku festivals|1=Nigori}} for festivals where doburoku is served. *[[Omiki]] *{{ill|Mass (liturgy)|en|Mass (liturgy)|qid=Q6783971|lt=Mass (liturgy)|1=Mass (liturgy)}} *[[Sake]] *{{ill|Oktoberfest|en|Oktoberfest|qid=Q102400|lt=Oktoberfest|1=Oktoberfest}} ==References== {{Reflist|qq=}}
[[en:Doburoku Matsuri]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:どぶろく祭り]] {{wikidata link|Q23997826}} [[Category:pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Rituals]] [[Category:Sake]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
